questions.question: Correct the following statement and rewrite: Stem absorbs water and minerals from the soil.

solution_data.solution: Roots absorb water and minerals from the soil.

questions.question: Correct the following statement and rewrite: Leaves hold the plant upright.
Correct the following statement and rewrite: Roots conduct water to the leaves.

solution_data.solution: The stem conducts water to the leaves.

questions.question: Correct the following statement and rewrite: The number of petals and stamens in a flower is always equal.

solution_data.solution: The number of petals and sepals in a flower may be equal or different in different plants.

questions.question: Correct the following statement and rewrite: If the sepals of a flower are joined together, its petals are also joined together.

solution_data.solution: If the sepals of a flower are joined together, then its petals may or may not be joined together.

questions.question: Correct the following statement and rewrite: If the petals of a flower are joined together, then the pistil is joined to the petal.

solution_data.solution: If the petals of a flower are joined together, then the pistil is not necessarily joined to the petal.

solution_data.solution:
1. Leaf :

questions.question: Can you find a plant in your house or in your neighborhood, which has a long but weak stem? Write its name. In which category will you place it?

solution_data.solution: Yes, Money plant. It is a climber.

questions.question: What is the function of a stem?

solution_data.solution:
Following are function of stem:
i) Support: It supports branches, leaves, flowers, fruits and buds.
ii) Transportation: It helps in the transportation of water and mineral salts absorbed by the roots of the plant and helps in supply of food the leaves to various parts of the plant such a roots and fruits.
iii) Food storage: The stem also functions as the storehouse of food materials as in the case of potato, onion, garlic, ginger.

questions.question:
Which of the following leaves have reticulate venation?
Wheat, tulsi, maize, grass, coriander (dhania), China rose
If a plant has fibrous root, what type of venation do its leaves have?

solution_data.solution: The plants having leaves with parallel venation have fibrous roots.

questions.question: If a plant has leaves with reticulate venation, what kind of roots will it have?

solution_data.solution: The plants having leaves with reticulate have tap roots.

questions.question: Is it possible for you to find out whether a plant has taproot or fibrous roots by looking at the impression of its leaf on a sheet of paper?

solution_data.solution:
Yes, we can recognize whether a plant has taproot or fibrous roots by looking at the impression of its leaves on a sheet of paper. To get an impression, we put a leaf under a white paper. Hold the pencil tip sideways and rub it on the portion of the paper having a leaf below. You get the impression of the leaf with some lines on it. These lines on the leaf are called veins.

  1.  The design made by veins of a leaf is called leaf venation.
  2. If we get the net-like design on both sides of the midrib, then the leaf has reticulate venation.
  3.  If we get lines parallel to each other, then the leaf has parallel venation.

questions.question: What are the parts of a flower?

solution_data.solution:
There are four main parts of a flower. They are as follows:
Petals, sepals, stamen (male reproductive organ), and Pistil (Female reproductive organ).

questions.question:
From the following plants, which of them have flowers?
Grass, maize, wheat, chilli, tomato, tulsi, peepal, shisham, banyan, mango, jamun, guava, pomegranate, papaya, banana, lemon, sugarcane, potato, groundnut.

solution_data.solution: Plants with flowers are maize, wheat, chilli, tomato, tulsi, pipal, shisham, banyan, mango, jamun, guava, pomegranate, papaya, banana, lemon, potato.

questions.question: Name the part of plant which produces food. Name the process.

solution_data.solution: Part of plant that produces food is leaves. It contains chlorophyll that traps the solar energy of sun. Chlorophyll is present inside the chloroplast. This process is called photosynthesis.

questions.question: In which part of a flower, you will find the ovary?

solution_data.solution: Pistil. Ovary is the lowermost part of pistil.

questions.question: Name two plants in which one has joined sepals and the other has separate sepals.

solution_data.solution:
• Plants with joined sepals are Datura and Tomato.
  • Plants with separate sepals are Lotus and Mustard.
